name: Forrest Gump
description: The 1994 Robert Zemeckis film that swept the Oscars, starring Tom Hanks as the simple man with a front-row seat to American history.

questions.q: Who stars as the title character in Forrest Gump?

source.notes: Hanks won his second Oscar for the role.

questions.q: Who directed Forrest Gump?

source.notes: Zemeckis also directed the Back to the Future trilogy.

questions.q: What is Forrest's famous line about a box of chocolates?
choices: 'Life is like a box of chocolates — you never know what you're gonna get.'
'Life is a chocolate — sweet and surprising.'
'A box of chocolates, like life, is full of twists.'
'Life is just chocolate — all of it.'

source.notes: A line his mother tells him.

questions.q: In which Southern U.S. state is Forrest's hometown of Greenbow located?

source.notes: Greenbow, Alabama is fictional.

questions.q: Which Vietnam-era military branch does Forrest serve in?

source.notes: Forrest is an infantryman.

questions.q: What sport does Forrest become a national star in — even meeting President Nixon?

source.notes: Part of the 'Ping-Pong Diplomacy' era.

questions.q: What is Forrest's lifelong love interest named?

source.notes: Played by Robin Wright.

questions.q: Who plays Lieutenant Dan Taylor, Forrest's Vietnam platoon leader?

source.notes: Sinise later founded the Gary Sinise Foundation for veterans.

questions.q: Which actress plays Forrest's mother, Mrs. Gump?

source.notes: Field is just 10 years older than Hanks.

questions.q: Who plays Forrest's army buddy Bubba, with whom he dreams of running a shrimp business?

source.notes: Benjamin Buford 'Bubba' Blue.

questions.q: Forrest Gump is based on a 1986 novel by which author?

source.notes: Groom, an Alabama native, published the novel in 1986.

questions.q: Who composed the Oscar-nominated score that includes the famous 'feather' theme?

source.notes: Silvestri also scored Back to the Future with Zemeckis.

questions.q: Forrest and his mother meet which segregationist governor 'blocking' the schoolhouse door?

source.notes: 1963 'Stand in the Schoolhouse Door' at Alabama.

questions.q: Which legendary University of Alabama football coach appears in Forrest's storyline?

source.notes: Bryant had passed away in 1983.

questions.q: Which early-career Elvis moves are based on young Forrest's leg-brace hobbling?

source.notes: Young Elvis stays at Forrest's mother's boardinghouse in one scene.

questions.q: What does Forrest famously run across America, growing out his hair and beard?

source.notes: About 3 years, 2 months, and 14 days of running.

questions.q: Which small piece of nature bookends the film in both the opening and closing shot?

source.notes: A white feather drifts into and out of frame.

questions.q: The film uses CGI to insert Forrest into footage of historical figures. Which was NOT one of them?

source.notes: Forrest also 'meets' John Lennon on Dick Cavett.

questions.q: Which stock company makes Forrest and Lieutenant Dan millionaires in the film?

source.notes: Forrest calls the fruit company 'some kind of apple.'

questions.q: Forrest Gump won how many Academy Awards in 1995?

source.notes: Best Picture, Director, Actor, Adapted Screenplay, Film Editing, Visual Effects.

questions.q: Which historic movement does Jenny become involved with and which keeps her away from Forrest?

source.notes: Jenny's arc covers flower power through disco and decline.

questions.q: Which Quentin Tarantino crime film did Forrest Gump beat for the 1995 Best Picture Oscar?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed inaccurate 'Paramount Pictures-distributed' framing (Pulp Fiction was Miramax).
